steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks what can be inferred about Ramchand. The key word is ‘guiltily’ — Ramchand notices that all the other shop assistants are already in place. Guilt signals he feels he has fallen short of a standard.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Ramchand is remorseful about arriving to work later than other shop assistants he sees.: The word ‘guiltily’ directly supports remorse, and the other assistants being ‘already in place’ confirms he is late. ✓
  • Choice B — Ramchand is mesmerized by frying jalebi batter while walking to work as a shop assistant.: The jalebi detail describes the scene; nothing says Ramchand is captivated by it. ✗
  • Choice C — Ramchand is overwhelmed by a bazaar after seeing the chaos of other shop assistants selling goods.: The assistants have ‘fixed, attentive smiles’ — no chaos is described. ✗
  • Choice D — Ramchand is happy about being at work after seeing shop assistants who are satisfied with their jobs.: Ramchand feels guilty, not happy. ✗
That is the correct answer.
